data.title: Cloud-based Infrastructure
data.description: Adobe utilizes a cloud-first architecture to deliver its services, leveraging multiple data centers across the globe.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes AWS and Azure for cloud hosting, with microservices architecture for scalability.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A CDN is employed to enhance the speed and performance of content delivery to users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Akami and Cloudflare CDNs for load balancing and caching.

data.title: API-driven Connectivity
data.description: APIs are at the core of Adobe's integration capabilities across its product suite.
data.technical_spec: RESTful and GraphQL APIs for cross-application data sharing.

data.title: Modular Product Suites
data.description: Adobe's software solutions are built with a modular approach, allowing seamless integration with additional tools.
data.technical_spec: Component-based architecture using JavaScript frameworks like React.

data.title: Automated Deployment Pipelines
data.description: Continuous integration and deployment methodologies to ensure swift updates and feature rollouts.
data.technical_spec: Jenkins and Kubernetes used for CI/CD processes.
